response:
In C# you can measure the time it takes to send a UDP packet by using a stopwatch. This will tell you how long it took for the packet to be sent from your application to the network. However this does not measure the time it takes for the packet to reach the server. It only measures the time from when you start sending until the send operation completes. 

To do this you can use the System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch class. Start the stopwatch before sending the packet and stop it after the send method returns. This will give you the time taken for the send operation. 

Keep in mind that UDP is connectionless and does not guarantee delivery. The send operation may complete quickly but the packet may not reach the server. There is no built in way to measure one way latency in UDP without the server responding. 

If you need to measure one way latency you would have to use a different protocol like TCP or implement a custom solution with UDP where the server sends back a timestamp. This allows you to calculate the round trip time and estimate one way latency. 

In summary you can measure the time it takes to send a UDP packet but not the time it takes to reach the server. The stopwatch can help you with the send time.
